enonce.texte:
Read the following article and identify its different parts.

THE DANGERS OF SOCIAL MEDIA

By Marie Claire, Form 5 Student

Social media has become an integral part of our daily lives. Platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp allow us to connect with friends and family around the world. However, behind the screen, there are serious dangers that many young people ignore.

Firstly, cyberbullying is one of the most common problems on social media. Many teenagers receive hurtful messages or are excluded from online groups. This can lead to depression, anxiety, and even suicide in extreme cases.

Secondly, addiction to social media is a growing concern. Young people spend hours scrolling through their feeds, neglecting their studies, physical activities, and real-life relationships. This addiction affects their mental health and academic performance.

Thirdly, fake news spreads quickly on social media. Many users share information without verifying its accuracy, which can cause panic, misinformation, and social unrest.

In conclusion, while social media offers many benefits, we must use it responsibly. Parents, teachers, and the government should work together to educate young people about the dangers and promote safe online habits.

questions.texte: Identify the title of the article.
questions.reponse: The title is 'THE DANGERS OF SOCIAL MEDIA'.

questions.texte: Who is the author of the article ?
questions.reponse: The author is Marie Claire, a Form 5 student.

questions.texte: How many main points does the author present in the body of the article ?
questions.reponse: The author presents three main points: cyberbullying, addiction, and fake news.

questions.texte: What is the purpose of the conclusion in this article ?
questions.reponse: The conclusion summarizes the main points and gives a recommendation (use social media responsibly and educate young people).

questions.texte: What type of language does the author use ? (formal/informal) Justify.
questions.reponse: The author uses formal language because the article addresses a serious topic, uses clear arguments, and is written for a general audience.

enonce.texte:
You are a journalist for your school magazine. Write an article of about 200-250 words on one of the following topics:

  • Option A: The importance of volunteering in your community
  • Option B: The dangers of drug abuse among teenagers
  • Option C: How to stay safe on the road

Your article should include:

  • A catchy title
  • An introduction that presents the topic
  • A body with at least 3 main points (use linking words: firstly, secondly, thirdly, in addition, moreover, finally)
  • A conclusion that summarizes and gives a recommendation

questions.texte: Write a complete article on your chosen topic.
questions.reponse:
Model Answer (Option A - Volunteering):

VOLUNTEERING: BUILDING A BETTER COMMUNITY

By a Student Journalist

Volunteering is one of the most rewarding activities a young person can engage in. It involves giving your time and energy to help others without expecting payment. In our communities, volunteering plays a vital role in addressing social problems and improving the quality of life.

Firstly, volunteering helps to solve community problems. Many neighborhoods face issues such as poverty, pollution, and illiteracy. Volunteers organize clean-up campaigns, teach children, and distribute food to the needy. These actions make a real difference in people's lives.

Secondly, volunteering develops important life skills. Young volunteers learn teamwork, communication, and leadership. They also gain a sense of responsibility and self-confidence. These skills are valuable for their future careers and personal development.

Thirdly, volunteering promotes social cohesion. When people from different backgrounds work together for a common cause, they build trust and friendship. This unity strengthens the community and reduces conflicts.

In conclusion, volunteering is a powerful tool for positive change. I strongly encourage every student to join a volunteer organization. Remember, even small acts of kindness can have a big impact. Start today and be the change you want to see in your community!

intitule: Article Structure - Putting Ideas in Order

enonce.texte:
The following sentences belong to an article about online shopping. Put them in the correct order to form a well-structured article.

Sentences :

  • A. In conclusion, online shopping is a convenient option, but we must be cautious.
  • B. Secondly, you can compare prices easily and find the best deals.
  • C. Online shopping has become very popular in recent years.
  • D. However, there are also risks such as fraud and delivery problems.
  • E. Firstly, it is convenient because you can shop from home at any time.
  • F. Therefore, it is important to buy from trusted websites and read reviews.
  • G. This article will discuss the advantages and disadvantages of online shopping.

questions.texte: Reorder the sentences to form a coherent article.
questions.reponse:
Correct order: C, G, E, B, D, F, A

Explanation:
- C: Introduction with general statement
- G: Presentation of the article's purpose
- E: First advantage
- B: Second advantage
- D: Introduction of disadvantages
- F: Advice/recommendation
- A: Conclusion

intitule: Identifying Facts vs Opinions in an Article

enonce.texte:
Read the following excerpts from an article about road safety and identify whether each sentence is a fact or an opinion.

1. Road accidents are the leading cause of death among young people aged 15-29 in Cameroon.

2. I believe that the government should install more traffic lights in cities.

3. Wearing a seat belt reduces the risk of death by 45% in a car crash.

4. It is unacceptable that many drivers do not respect the speed limit.

5. According to the World Health Organization, 1.3 million people die in road accidents each year.

questions.texte: Identify whether each sentence is a fact or an opinion.
questions.reponse:
1. Fact (statistical information)
2. Opinion (personal belief: 'I believe')
3. Fact (statistical information)
4. Opinion (personal judgment: 'unacceptable')
5. Fact (reference to a reliable source)

enonce.texte:
You are a reporter for the school newspaper. Your editor has asked you to write a short article about the problem of littering and waste in your school or community.

Write only the following parts :

  • A catchy title (headline) for the article
  • An introduction paragraph (5-7 sentences) that presents the topic and captures the reader's attention

questions.texte: Write a headline and an introduction for your article.
questions.reponse:
Model Answer :

Headline : OUR SCHOOL IS NOT A DUMP !

Introduction : Walk into our school compound today and you will see plastic bottles, food wrappers, and paper scattered everywhere. Littering has become a serious problem in our school, and it affects our health, our environment, and our pride. Every morning, the janitors spend hours cleaning, but by lunchtime, the waste is back. This is not just an eyesore; it is a danger to our health, as waste attracts insects and rodents. It is time for all of us to take responsibility. In this article, I will discuss the causes of this problem and propose practical solutions.

enonce.texte: Match the following words with their definitions. These words are often used in articles.

questions.texte: Match each word to its definition.
questions.reponse:
1. Editorial → A. An article expressing the opinion of the newspaper's editor.
2. Column → B. A regular article in a newspaper or magazine by the same writer.
3. Feature → C. A special article in a newspaper or magazine, often about a specific topic.
4. Headline → D. The title of an article, usually in large letters.
5. Byline → E. The line that gives the author's name.
6. Source → F. A person, document, or website that provides information.
7. Quote → G. The exact words spoken by someone, used in an article.
